LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND'S MOTION TO DISQUALIFY OPPOSING COUNSEL AND FOR OTHER APPROPRIATE RELIEF - DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO DISQUALIFY PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL IS DENIED. THE COURT FINDS THAT DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL WAS NOT DILIGENT IN ATTEMPTING TO RETRIEVE THE DOCUMENTS FROM PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL. FURTHERMORE, DEFENDANT DOES NOT PROVIDE SUFFICIENT EXPLANATION AS TO THE INADVERTENCE IN THE RELEASE OF THE DOCUMENTS OR THE DELAY IN RETRIEVING THEM. MOREOVER, THE FACT THAT THE DEFENDANTS DID NOT ALERT THE COURT, IN THEIR REPLY BRIEF TO THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION, TO THE FACT THAT THE DOCUMENTS CITED IN PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION CONTAINED THESE ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGED DOCUMENT RAISES QUESTIONS AS TO THE MOTIVE BEHIND THE BRINGING OF THIS MOTION FOLLOWING THE BRIEFING ON THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION TO STRIKE. IN ADDITION, THE COURT INDICATED DOCUMENTS TO BE RETURNED TO COUNSEL. PARTIES TO MEET & CONFER. IF ANY DISPUTE, WRITTEN MOTION TO BE BROUGHT BEFORE THE COURT TO RESOLVE OUTSTANDING ISSUES RE CLIENT-ATTORNEY PRIVILEGE/DISPUTED DOCUMENTS. ANY DISPUTED DOCUMENTS NOT TO BE USED. AS FOR THE SLAPP MOTION CURRENTLY SET FOR 07/11/11, THE COURT SET A FURTHER BRIEFING SCHEDULE: SUPPLEMENTAL OPPOSITION DUE 08/01/11, SUPPLEMENTAL REPLY DUE 08/08/11, HEARING SET FOR 08/12/11. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: SONIA WONG, CSR #11288

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND'S MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFFS' 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE (ANTI-SLAPP) THE 1ST, 2ND, 3RD, 4TH, 6TH, 9TH AND 10TH CAUSES OF ACTION IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS SUCCESSFULLY ESTABLISHED THAT THESE CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE COMPLAINT CONTAIN ALLEGATIONS OF "NONINCIDENTAL PROTECTED ACTIVITY AS WELL AS NONPROTECTED ACTIVITY AND, THEREFORE, IMPLICATES CCP 425.16. HAIGHT ASHBURRY FREE CLINIC INC. V. HAPPENING HOUSE VENTURES, (2010) 184 CAL.APP.4TH 1539. HOWEVER, PLAINTIFF CAN SHOW A LIKELIHOOD OF PREVAILING. FIRST, LITIGATION PRIVILEGE DOES NOT BAR THE ENTIRE ACTION. PLAINTIFF HAS SHOWN THAT DEFENDANT'S STATEMENTS WERE NOT REASONABLY RELATED TO LITIGATION. DEFENDANT'S DEFINITION OF "LITIGATION" AS ALL OF THE 400 PLUS WORKER'S COMPENSATION CLAIMS IS UNDULY OVERBROAD. SECOND, DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHOW HOW THE STATEMENT OF ALCORN TO GET A NEW DOCTOR OR THE STATEMENTS TO DR. DOKHANCHIFAR TO STAY AWAY FROM PLAINTIFF, NOT REFER PATIENTS TO HIM, AND THAT PLAINTIFF WAS A "CROOK" ARE RELATED TO LITIGATION OR ACHIEVED AN OBJECTIVE OF LITIGATION. SEE NGUYEN V. PROTON TECHNOLOGY CORP., (1999) 69 CAL.APP.4TH 140. MOREOVER, INS. CODE 1877.5 DOES NOT IMMUNIZE DEFENDANT. THAT STATUTE HAS A GOOD FAITH REQUIREMENT AND DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHOW THAT THESE COMMUNICATIONS WERE PART OF AN INVESTIGATION. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER.

PLAINTIFFS VATCHE CABAYAN M.D. AND VATCHE CABAYAN MEDICAL CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS AND MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT FOR CONTINUOUS ABUSES OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS AND ONGOING VIOLATIONS OF COURT ORDERS AND/OR FOR ORDER COMPELLING FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION IS GRANTED. WITHIN 15 DAYS OF SERVICE OF THE ENTRY OF ORDER STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND SHALL: (1) SERVE VERIFIED SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION 8, 10, 14, 15, 16 AND 17, WITHOUT OBJECTIONS BASED UPON THE INVESTIGATION PRIVILEGE, AND EXPLICITLY STATING THAT ALL NONPRIVILEGED DOCUMENTS RESPONSIVE TO THOSE REQUESTS HAVE BEEN PRODUCED AND THAT NO DOCUMENTS HAVE BEEN WITHHELD BASED UPON A CLAIM OF THE INVESTIGATION PRIVILEGE; (2) PRODUCE ANY NON-PRIVILEGED DOCUMENTS IN THE "A" AND "D" RANGE THAT HAVE NOT BEEN PREVIOUSLY PRODUCED; (3) REVIEW ALL OF THE FUND'S DOCUMENTS PREVIOUSLY PRODUCED FOR EMAILS THAT ARE MISSING ATTACHMENTS, AND TO PRODUCE ANY SUCH EMAILS AGAIN WITH THEIR ATTACHMENTS; (4) RE-EXAMINE EVERY DOCUMENT LISTED IN THE FUND'S PRIVILEGE TO ENSURE THAT IT HAS NOT BEEN WITHHELD ON A CLAIM OF INVESTIGATION PRIVILEGE, AND PRODUCE UNREDACTED COPIES OF ANY THAT HAD EITHER BEEN REDACTED OR WITHHELD ON THE BASIS OF A CLAIM OF INVESTIGATION PRIVILEGE; AND (5) SERVE A NEW PRIVILEGE LOG ON PLAINTIFFS' COUNSEL THAT IDENTIFIES AUTHORS, SENDERS, OR RECIPIENTS WHO ARE ATTORNEYS AS SUCH, EXPLICITLY STATES THE PRIVILEGE BEING CLAIMED FOR EACH DOCUMENT, AND PROVIDES ADEQUATE DESCRIPTION OF THE DOCUMENTS TO FULFILL THE FUND'S INITIAL BURDEN IN CLAIMING ATTORNEY-CLIENT OR ATTORNEY WORK PRODUCT PRIVILEGE. MONETARY SANCTIONS OF $ 5,000 ARE AWARDED TO DR. CABAYAN AND AGAINST STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND. THE COURT ADDS A PAYMENT DUE DATE OF NOVEMBER 26, 2012. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Post Judgment Attorney Fees By Plaintiffs And Cross Defendants Vatche Cabayan, M.D. And V. Cabayan Med. Corp.
Off Calendar Sep-06-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Michael J. Hassen, Esq. of Jeffer Mangels Butler & Mitchell LLP, Tel. 415.398.8080, for plaintiff and moving party;
Eric P. Jones, Esq. of State Compensation Insurance Fund, Tel. 925.523.5186, for defendants and opposing party.

RULING: No party contested the court's tentative ruling on PLAINTIFFS VATCHE CABAYAN M.D. and VATCHE CABAYAN MEDICAL CORPORATION's Motion For Post Judgment Attorney Fees, which was adopted as follows: Plaintiffs Vatche Cabayan and V. Cabayan Med. Corp.'s motion for post-judgment attorney fees is granted in substantial part. Plaintiffs are awarded attorney fees in the amount of $126,636.50 against defendant State Compensation Insurance Fund. State Fund's request that this motion be deferred until resolution of its appeal is denied. The parties have fully briefed the merits of the motion and thus judicial economy is best served by deciding the motion now. Nor will delay enhance thoughtful adjudication of the motion. Nor is State Fund prejudiced by deciding the motion now. If its appeal is successful, the award of fees to plaintiffs will be reversed. The only substantive dispute on this motion is the hourly rates sought by the three timekeepers for whom recovery of fees are sought by plaintiffs. The billing rates of the two attorneys exceed what is reasonable and the norm for the Bay Area for what was not particularly complex or involved legal work. Moreover, the great majority of the legal work was done by an attorney with a very high billing rate, thus precluding cost efficiency in utilizing the services of an attorney with a much lower billing rate, which would unlikely to have impacted the results obtained. Given the nature of the work performed, which the court is fully aware of due to having read all the papers, presided over the hearings and decided the CCP 664.6 motion, the court's view is that the maximum reasonable hourly rates for the work performed by Mr. Hassen and Mr. Doyle are $700 and $550 respectively. The hourly rate for Ms. Patts is within the realm of reasonable. (150.7 x $700) + (37.6 x $550) +$466.50 = $126,636.50. The prevailing party shall submit a proposed order that repeats verbatim the Court's ruling and complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, rhunter@sftc.org.

Plaintiff Vatche Cabayan M.D., Vatche Cabayan Medical Corporation Motion To Vacate Dismissal And, Or Enforce Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Apr-17-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. MICAHEL HASSEN OF THE JEFFER MANGEELS LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-415-398-8080 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. ERIC P. JONES OF THE STATE FUND, TEL. NO. 1-925-523-5186 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiffs Vatche Cabayan, M.D. and V. Cabayan Medical Corporation's motion to enforce settlement agreement is granted. The language 'State Fund's Medical Provider Network' is objectively broad enough to encompass an MPN that is affiliated with defendant State Compensation Insurance Fund even though it is not administered by the State Fund. Consequently, the settlement agreement has not been fully performed and this court retains continuing jurisdiction over the action per CCP 664.6. (Wackeen v. Malis (2002) 97 Cal.App.4th 429, 439.) The State Fund is required to include Dr. Cabayan as an authorized medical provider in the State Fund MPN by Harbor Health pursuant to the settlement agreement. The State Fund's argument that it could have terminated Dr. Cabayan from the MPN lacks merit because there is no evidence that it ever did so. Nor does Civil Code section 3390 preclude enforcement of the settlement agreement. The Services Agreement between the State Fund and Harbor Health does not remove the authority from the State Fund to include Dr. Cabayan in the State Fund MPN administered by Harbor Health. The Services Agreement states: "Both Parties will collaborate and agree upon specific performance and compliance standards for all physician members participating in the State Fund MPN." (Emphasis added.) Dr. Cabayan's objection to the State Fund's argument that enforcement of the settlement agreement is precluded by the personal services language of section 3390 is sustained. This argument was raised for the first time in the State Fund's opposition to third supplemental reply brief and Dr. Cabayan did not have an opportunity to reply per the briefing schedule established by this court's March 15, 2017 order. In all events, if the argument were considered, it would be rejected. The inclusion of a physician as an authorized medical provider in the MPN is not "employment" of the physician -- and certainly not "employment in a personal service" -- by either State Fund or Harbor Health. Defendant's counsel was given an opporunity to give an input until April 19 whether he is entitled to a statement of decision under CCP 664.6 and to the proposed order and judgment submited by the Plaintiff. Plaintiff to respond by April 20, 2017. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk

Off Calendar Oct-26-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Diane Peacock, Esq. of Physicians' Advocates, Tel. 510.841.7500, for plaintiff and moving party;
Steven D. Roland, Esq. and Jia-Ming Shang, Esq. of Sedgwick LLP, Tel. 415.781.7900, for defendant State Compensation Insurance Fund.

The matter was argued. After arguments, the court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiffs Vatche Cabayan M.D. And Vatche Cabayan Medical Corporation'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant For Continuous Abuses Of The Discovery Process And Ongoing Violations Of Court Orders And/Or For Order Compelling Further Responses To Requests For Production is Granted. Within 15 days of service of the entry of order State Compensation Insurance Fund shall: (1) serve verified supplemental responses to requests for production 8, 10, 14, 15, 16 and 17, without objections based upon the investigation privilege, and explicitly stating that all nonprivileged documents responsive to those requests have been produced and that no documents have been withheld based upon a claim of the investigation privilege; (2) produce any non-privileged documents in the "A" and "D" range that have not been previously produced; (3) review all of the Fund's documents previously produced for emails that are missing attachments, and to produce any such emails again with their attachments; (4) re-examine every document listed in the Fund's privilege to ensure that it has not been withheld on a claim of investigation privilege, and produce unredacted copies of any that had either been redacted or withheld on the basis of a claim of investigation privilege; and (5) Serve a new privilege log on Plaintiffs' counsel that identifies authors, senders, or recipients who are attorneys as such, explicitly states the privilege being claimed for each document, and provides adequate description of the documents to fulfill the Fund's initial burden in claiming attorney-client or attorney work product privilege. Monetary sanctions of $ 5,000 are awarded to Dr. Cabayan and against State Compensation Insurance Fund. The court adds a payment due date of November 26, 2012. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Tara A. Lohman, CSR #13428 (Tel. 510.451.1580)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-12-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND'S MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFFS' 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. No appearances. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant's Special Motion to Strike (Anti-SLAPP) the 1st, 2nd, 3rd, 4th, 6th, 9th and 10th causes of action is denied. Defendant has successfully established that these causes of action in the complaint contain allegations of "nonincidental protected activity as well as nonprotected activity and, therefore, implicates CCP 425.16. Haight Ashburry Free Clinic Inc. v. Happening House Ventures, (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1539. However, Plaintiff can show a likelihood of prevailing. First, litigation privilege does not bar the entire action. Plaintiff has shown that Defendant's statements were not reasonably related to litigation. Defendant's definition of "litigation" as all of the 400 plus worker's compensation claims is unduly overbroad. Second, Defendant fails to show how the statement of Alcorn to get a new Doctor or the statements to Dr. Dokhanchifar to stay away from Plaintiff, not refer patients to him, and that Plaintiff was a "crook" are related to litigation or achieved an objective of litigation. See Nguyen v. Proton Technology Corp., (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 140. Moreover, Ins. Code 1877.5 does not immunize Defendant. That statute has a good faith requirement and Defendant fails to show that these communications were part of an investigation. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

Notice Of Moiton And Moiton To Disqualify Opposing Counsel And For Other Appropriate Relief
Off Calendar Jul-08-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND'S MOTION TO DISQUALIFY OPPOSING COUNSEL AND FOR OTHER APPROPRIATE RELIEF.

APPEARANCES: Jane Luciano, Esq. of Physicians' Advocates 510/841-7500 for pltfs & opposing parties; William D. Hale, Esq. of State Compensation Insurance Fund 323/526-2129 for deft & moving party.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Defendant's Motion to Disqualify Plaintiff's Counsel is denied. The Court finds that Defendant's counsel was not diligent in attempting to retrieve the documents from Plaintiff's counsel. Furthermore, defendant does not provide sufficient explanation as to the inadvertence in the release of the documents or the delay in retrieving them. Moreover, the fact that the Defendants did not alert the Court, in their Reply brief to the Anti-SLAPP Motion, to the fact that the documents cited in Plaintiff's Opposition contained these Attorney-Client Privileged Document raises questions as to the motive behind the bringing of this motion following the briefing on the Anti-SLAPP Motion to Strike.
